meta.source_note: 补充 JSON 导入；页面默认不展示 source_url/source_type/confidence。

ai_models.key_capabilities: 0.5B, 1.5B, 7B, 57B-A14B, 72B variants; strong multilingual and coding capabilities; first Apache 2.0 license for most sizes
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Broad size range enables deployment from edge to cloud; 72B and MoE variants require significant inference compute; open weights drove ecosystem adoption

ai_models.key_capabilities: 0.5B, 1.5B, 3B, 7B, 14B, 32B, 72B variants; significantly improved reasoning, coding, and instruction following; dense and specialized models
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Very broad model family; larger variants trained on substantial GPU clusters; open weights enabled massive downstream deployment

ai_models.key_capabilities: Preview of reasoning model; 32B parameters; strong math and coding reasoning; later merged into Qwen3 architecture
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Inference-time reasoning increased per-query compute; preview status limited scale

ai_models.key_capabilities: Large-scale MoE model; strong intelligence at scale; closed API model on DashScope platform
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Massive MoE architecture; very high training compute; closed API concentrates inference demand on Alibaba Cloud infrastructure

ai_models.key_capabilities: Production reasoning model; 32B parameters; strong performance on math, science, and coding benchmarks
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Efficient reasoning model; moderate per-query compute relative to larger models; high adoption in developer community

ai_models.key_capabilities: Hybrid reasoning architecture; 0.6B-32B dense, 30B-A3B and 235B-A22B MoE; think mode and quick response mode; all Apache 2.0
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: 235B-A22B MoE is massive; hybrid reasoning allows compute scaling per query; open weights drove global deployment; very large training run

ai_models.key_capabilities: Expanded to 201 languages; native multimodal (text+image+video); improved reasoning and agentic capabilities
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Multimodal training and inference significantly increased compute demands; massive multilingual training required large GPU clusters

ai_models.key_capabilities: Gated DeltaNet hybrid architecture; thought retention; 262K context expandable to 1M; strong reasoning
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Novel architecture may change compute characteristics; very long context requires significant memory; 27B size enables efficient serving

ai_models.key_capabilities: Closed-source reasoning model; 1M token context; native extended thinking; strongest Qwen model to date
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Massive training run; very high inference compute per query; 1M context requires enormous memory bandwidth

ai_models.key_capabilities: Multimodal agent flagship; vision+language understanding; deep reasoning; tool use and autonomous iteration
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Agentic capabilities increase per-session compute significantly; multimodal processing adds compute overhead; large model size

ai_models.key_capabilities: Most capable Claude 3 model; near-human comprehension on complex tasks; strong coding, reasoning, and creative writing; 200K context
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Largest model in Claude 3 family; high training and inference compute requirements; drove demand for GPU clusters for serving

ai_models.key_capabilities: Balanced intelligence and speed; 2x faster than Claude 2; strong reasoning and multilingual capabilities; 200K context
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Mid-tier compute requirements; balanced for enterprise deployment at scale; significant inference volume

ai_models.key_capabilities: Fastest and most affordable Claude 3 model; 3x faster than peers; 21K tokens/sec for prompts under 32K; strong vision
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Optimized for low-latency, high-volume inference; minimal per-token compute enabled massive deployment scale

ai_models.key_capabilities: First Claude 3.5 model; outperformed Claude 3 Opus on most benchmarks; strong coding and reasoning; artifact capabilities
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: More efficient than Claude 3 Opus despite better performance; improved training efficiency reduced compute per capability unit

ai_models.key_capabilities: Most capable Claude 3.5 model; superior coding and reasoning; strong agentic capabilities; 200K context
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Highest compute in Claude 3.5 family; large model required significant training infrastructure

ai_models.key_capabilities: Fast, cost-effective model matching Claude 3 Opus intelligence; optimized for high-volume enterprise applications
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Highly efficient inference; very low per-token compute; enabled massive scale deployment for latency-sensitive apps

ai_models.key_capabilities: Next generation flagship; new standards for coding, advanced reasoning, and agentic workflows; extended thinking capabilities
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Massive training run; very high inference compute per query; agentic workflows increase session compute significantly

ai_models.key_capabilities: Balanced next-gen model; strong coding and reasoning; more efficient than Opus 4; suitable for enterprise deployment
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Balanced compute profile; efficient training and inference; strong enterprise adoption drove inference chip demand

ai_models.key_capabilities: Latest flagship model; enhanced reasoning and coding capabilities; improved safety and reliability
ai_models.next_generation: Date approximate based on system cards
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Largest Anthropic model to date; significant training compute on GPU clusters; high inference compute requirements

ai_models.key_capabilities: Faster, more efficient version of ERNIE 4.0; improved reasoning and generation speed; optimized for enterprise applications
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Optimized inference stack; reduced per-token latency enabled higher throughput and lower serving costs

ai_models.key_capabilities: New family of large-scale foundation models; improved reasoning, coding, and multilingual capabilities; multimodal understanding
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Large-scale training run; very high parameter count; significant compute requirements for both training and inference

ai_models.key_capabilities: Reasoning model released alongside ERNIE 4.5; strong performance on math, coding, and logic; chain-of-thought capabilities
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Inference-time reasoning increases per-query compute; training required reinforcement learning on large GPU clusters

ai_models.key_capabilities: Open-sourced version of ERNIE 4.5; released to promote ecosystem development and research
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Open weights enable local deployment and fine-tuning; inference demand shifts from centralized to distributed compute

ai_models.key_capabilities: Updated reasoning model with improved chain-of-thought and multimodal reasoning; stronger math and coding performance
ai_models.next_generation: Date from third-party analysis; limited official confirmation
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Enhanced reasoning increases inference compute; continued training on large clusters

ai_models.key_capabilities: Next-generation foundation model; unified multimodal understanding and generation; agentic capabilities; strongest ERNIE model to date
ai_models.next_generation: Date from third-party analysis; limited official confirmation
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Massive training infrastructure; very high compute for multimodal training; agentic workflows increase session compute

ai_models.key_capabilities: Long-context model with up to 1M token context window; strong multimodal understanding across text, code, images, audio, video
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Massive context window required significant memory and compute; drove demand for high-memory GPU/TPU configurations for serving

ai_models.key_capabilities: Fast, cost-efficient model; 1M context window; optimized for high-volume applications; strong multimodal capabilities
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Optimized for efficient serving; lower per-token compute than Pro enabled massive scale deployment; drove inference chip volume

ai_models.key_capabilities: Next generation of open models; 9B, 27B, 2B variants; significantly improved performance over Gemma 1; efficient architecture
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Designed for efficient inference on consumer and cloud hardware; reduced per-token compute drove broader deployment on TPU and GPU

ai_models.key_capabilities: Ultra-lightweight 2B parameter model; strong performance for size; designed for on-device and edge deployment
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Minimal compute requirements; enabled AI on low-power devices; reduced reliance on cloud inference for simple tasks

ai_models.key_capabilities: New AI model designed for agentic era; native tool use and multimodal capabilities; improved reasoning and coding
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Large training run on Google TPU clusters; agentic capabilities increased average inference compute per session

ai_models.key_capabilities: First thinking model in Gemini family; experimental reasoning model with visible chain-of-thought; fast and efficient
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Thinking capability increased per-query compute; experimental status limited scale but demonstrated new inference paradigm

ai_models.key_capabilities: Workhorse model with low latency and cost-efficiency; strong performance for enterprise applications; multimodal
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Optimized for high-volume inference; cost-efficient design drove massive deployment scale and TPU/GPU inference demand

ai_models.key_capabilities: Lowest-cost Gemini model for high-volume use cases; maintained quality while minimizing latency and cost
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Minimal per-token compute; designed for maximum throughput and lowest cost inference; high volume drives chip demand

ai_models.key_capabilities: Most advanced model for complex tasks; state-of-the-art coding and reasoning; 2M token context window
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Highest compute per token in Gemini 2.0 family; large model size required significant training and inference infrastructure

ai_models.key_capabilities: Open models with multimodal capabilities; 1B, 4B, 12B, 27B variants; strong performance for size class; 128K-140K context
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Efficient open-weight models for local and cloud deployment; broad parameter range enables deployment across hardware tiers

ai_models.key_capabilities: First thinking model in Gemini 2.5 family; state-of-the-art on benchmarks; #1 on LMArena; strong reasoning and code capabilities
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Thinking models require significantly more inference compute per query; training with RL drove large TPU cluster demand

ai_models.key_capabilities: Workhorse thinking model with low latency and cost-efficiency; incorporates thinking capabilities while maintaining speed
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Balanced compute design; efficient thinking architecture enabled broader deployment of reasoning capabilities

ai_models.key_capabilities: Stable version of most powerful Gemini model; adaptive thinking; state-of-the-art coding and reasoning performance
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Largest Gemini model to date; very high training compute on TPU v5p clusters; inference demands high-memory accelerators

ai_models.key_capabilities: Low-cost, high-performance Gemini 2.5 model; optimized for price-sensitive high-volume applications
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Minimal per-token compute; designed for maximum cost efficiency and throughput

ai_models.key_capabilities: Advanced open models for reasoning and agentic workflows; E2B, E4B, 31B, 26B A4B variants; built on latest architecture
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Larger variants require significant inference compute; agentic capabilities increase per-session compute demands

ai_models.key_capabilities: First Llama 3 generation; 8B and 70B variants; major improvements in reasoning, code, and multilingual capabilities over Llama 2; 8K context
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Large training run on ~16K H100 GPUs; open weights drove massive fine-tuning and deployment ecosystem;...

ai_models.key_capabilities: Largest open model at release; 8B, 70B, 405B variants; 128K context; first frontier-level open source model; multilingual support
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: 405B trained on over 16,000 NVIDIA H100 GPUs; massive compute investment estimated at hundreds of millions of dollars; drove significant H100 demand

ai_models.key_capabilities: Edge and mobile optimized models; 1B, 3B text-only; 11B, 90B vision models; lightweight for on-device deployment; 128K context
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Small models designed for edge inference on mobile/edge chips (Qualcomm, MediaTek, Arm); reduced cloud compute demand for edge use cases

ai_models.key_capabilities: 70B model with improved performance matching larger models; cost-efficient alternative to 405B; 128K context
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Efficient inference relative to performance; enabled high-volume deployment without 405B compute requirements

ai_models.key_capabilities: Native multimodal model; 17B active parameters, 16 experts; 10M token context window; efficient architecture
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: MoE architecture reduces active compute per token; extremely long context (10M) requires significant memory bandwidth; trained on large GPU cluster

ai_models.key_capabilities: Native multimodal model; 17B active parameters, 128 experts; strong performance across text, image, video; 1M context
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Larger MoE than Scout; higher training compute; significant inference demands for multimodal processing

ai_models.key_capabilities: Largest Llama 4 model; massive scale; used as teacher model for distilling Scout and Maverick; not openly released
ai_models.next_generation: Preview/teacher model only, not openly released
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Massive training compute; not publicly available so limited direct inference demand; drove training-phase chip demand

ai_models.key_capabilities: Meta's first closed-weights frontier model after Llama 2; API-only access; strongest Meta model to date; multimodal
ai_models.next_generation: First closed-weights model from Meta since Llama went open
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Closed model implies massive training investment; API-only serving drives concentrated inference compute demand; likely trained on very large GPU clusters

ai_models.key_capabilities: Flagship multimodal model reasoning across audio, vision, and text in real time; 2x faster than GPT-4 Turbo at half the price; native audio processing without separate pipelines
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Significantly more efficient than GPT-4 Turbo despite broader modality support; reduced API costs drove higher inference volume and chip demand for serving

ai_models.key_capabilities: High-performance small model replacing GPT-3.5 Turbo; strong on text intelligence and multimodal reasoning; 128K context
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Designed for cost-efficient inference at scale; high volume deployment increased demand for inference-optimized chips (H100, later Blackwell)

ai_models.key_capabilities: First reasoning model with chain-of-thought; thinks before answering; excels at math, science, coding
ai_models.next_generation: Replaced by o1 GA in Dec 2024, then o3/o4 series
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Massive increase in training compute vs GPT-4; inference-time scaling via longer thinking chains drove demand for both training and inference chips

ai_models.key_capabilities: Faster, cheaper reasoning model; 80% cheaper than o1-preview; competitive with o1 on STEM tasks
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Lower per-token compute than o1-preview but still higher than GPT-4o; enabled broader adoption of reasoning workloads

ai_models.key_capabilities: General availability of o1 reasoning model; full feature set including vision, tool use, and structured outputs; longer thinking chains than preview
ai_models.next_generation: Deprecated Feb 2026 per OpenAI release notes
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Higher inference compute per query than GPT-4o; training required large GPU clusters; accelerated demand for H100/H200 clusters

ai_models.key_capabilities: Cost-efficient reasoning model optimized for coding, math, and science; supports structured outputs and function calling
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Designed for efficient reasoning inference; lower cost than full o3 enabled broader deployment and higher query volumes

ai_models.key_capabilities: Research preview of strongest GPT model at the time; improved pattern recognition, creative insight, and emotional intelligence; reduced hallucinations
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Very large model with high compute requirements for both training and inference; preview status limited scale; scheduled retirement June 27 2026

ai_models.key_capabilities: Most powerful reasoning model at launch; excels at coding, math, science, and visual reasoning; tool use capabilities
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Highest inference compute of o-series at launch; very large training run; drove significant H100/Blackwell demand; retired Aug 2026

ai_models.key_capabilities: High-efficiency reasoning model; strong performance on coding and STEM; lower latency than o3
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Optimized for cost-efficient reasoning inference; high deployment volume increased inference chip demand; deprecated Feb 2026

ai_models.key_capabilities: Strong coding and instruction-following model; improved long-context comprehension; replacement for GPT-4o in many tasks
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Efficient frontier model; balanced training and inference compute requirements; drove continued demand for large training clusters

ai_models.key_capabilities: Enhanced reliability version of o3 for Pro users; stronger performance on math, science, and programming tasks
ai_models.next_generation: Added to ChatGPT Pro and API June 2025
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Higher compute per query than standard o3; targeted at high-value enterprise and research workloads

ai_models.key_capabilities: Open-weight 120B reasoning model released by OpenAI; first open model weights from OpenAI
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Open weights enable local deployment and fine-tuning; inference demand shifts from centralized APIs to distributed/local compute

ai_models.key_capabilities: Open-weight 20B reasoning model; smaller, more efficient variant of gpt-oss-120b
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Efficient for edge and local deployment; minimal per-token compute enables broad accessibility

ai_models.key_capabilities: Unified model architecture combining GPT and reasoning surfaces; single router determines when to use reasoning; major capability leap
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Massive training run unifying prior architectures; very high inference demand at launch; required significant expansion of serving infrastructure

ai_models.key_capabilities: Current OpenAI flagship model (API strings gpt-5.5, gpt-5.5-pro); major advances in reasoning, coding, and agentic capabilities
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Largest and most compute-intensive GPT model to date; training required massive GPU clusters; inference drives continued chip demand

ai_models.key_capabilities: Fast, efficient variant serving as default ChatGPT model; API string chat-latest; optimized for latency and cost
ai_models.next_generation: Updated May 28 2026 with improved response style
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Optimized for high-volume, low-latency inference; drives massive inference chip demand as default model for hundreds of millions of users

ai_models.key_capabilities: 314B parameter Mixture-of-Experts model; open-sourced under Apache 2.0; base model weights and architecture released
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Large MoE model (314B total, ~86B active); open sourcing drove replication and research demand for inference; trained on significant GPU infrastructure

ai_models.key_capabilities: Improved reasoning capabilities; 128,000 token context length; better coding and math performance
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Larger context required more memory per inference; training compute increased over Grok-1

ai_models.key_capabilities: Two new models: Grok-2 and Grok-2 mini; improved reasoning and tool use; image generation via Aurora partnership
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Significant training scale-up; inference deployment across X platform drove GPU demand; Aurora image model added multimodal compute

ai_models.key_capabilities: Flagship model at debut; exceptional reasoning and pre-training knowledge; released alongside Grok 3 mini
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Trained on 200,000 GPU 'Colossus' supercluster in Memphis; massive training run significantly drove NVIDIA H100/H200 demand; one of largest training clusters

ai_models.key_capabilities: World's smartest model at release per xAI; native tool use and real-time search integration; Auto mode
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Massive training run on expanded Colossus cluster; very high inference compute; real-time search increases per-query compute

ai_models.key_capabilities: Updated Grok 4 with Auto mode and manual selection; deployed across grok.com, X, and mobile apps
ai_models.compute_supply_chain_impact: Continued scaling of inference infrastructure; widespread deployment increased serving chip demand
